topic_title: What parameters are and why they matter
verdict: PASS — all five checks pass; two soft findings: (1) §6 Implementation contains zero inline citation markers despite carrying factual figures (memory sizes) that are derived from §5.4's cited content; (2) acronym 'GPU' is never spelled out on first use.

required_sections.detail: §3 Learning Objectives (5 numbered LOs, >50 words), §4 Introduction (3 paragraphs, >50 words), §5 Core Concepts (5 sub-sections, well >100 words), §10 Key Takeaways (5 bulleted items with citations, >50 words) — all present and substantial. No placeholder strings.

citation_density.detail: Primary [1] cited at least once — confirmed, cited extensively across §5, §7, §8, §10. §5 and §7 exceed 60% claim-coverage with [1]/[2]/[3] markers. §6 Implementation has zero inline markers (soft finding); the underlying figures are derived directly from §5.4's cited fp16/int8/int4 table, so no fabricated citations are present. No fake [N] markers detected.
soft_findings: §6 Implementation contains no inline [N] citation markers despite stating specific memory figures (14 GB, 7 GB, 4 GB) — these figures are already cited in §5.4 but are repeated without markers in §6.

sequence_drift.detail: All technical noun phrases resolve to either this topic's concepts_introduced (parameter/weight, weight matrix, bias, scaling law, quantisation, model size/GB, parameter count, emergent capability, fp16, int8, int4) or prior topics 3.1–3.4 (machine learning, deep learning, artificial neural network, LLM, Transformer architecture, pre-training/fine-tuning, hallucination, prompt, token, tokenisation, backpropagation, attention mechanism, inference, context window, next-token prediction). 'fine-tuning' referenced twice with explicit 'covered in a later topic' deferral — correctly scoped. 'model card' named in §6 with inline explanation; not a hard forward ref.
soft_findings: 'model card' used in §6 without a prior formal sequence introduction; adequately explained inline ('published by the model creators on platforms like Hugging Face').

length_sanity.detail: Estimated ~2480 words across all prose sections (§2–§10). Within the standard profile range of 1800–3500 words.

topic_scope.detail: Title boundary 'What parameters are and why they matter' respected throughout. Temperature/sampling not mentioned. Fine-tuning appears twice as named-and-deferred one-liners ('covered in a later topic') — within the allowed one-sentence pointer rule. RLHF not mentioned. RAG not mentioned. No concept is taught at depth beyond the headline scope.

beginner_readability.detail: All key terms defined on first use: parameter/weight (§5.1), weight matrix (§5.1), bias (§5.1), scaling laws (§5.3), emergent capability (§5.3), quantisation (§5.4), fp16/int8/int4 (§5.4 table). No jargon dumps (no paragraph introduces 3+ brand-new terms simultaneously). Register is plain and accessible; hedging phrases are minimal ('roughly speaking' appears once). Average sentence length well under 25 words.
soft_findings: undefined_terms: 'GPU' used in §5.5, §6, §7, §8 without spell-out on first use (Graphics Processing Unit). 'API' used in §6 and §8 without spell-out — however 'API' is a general computing abbreviation widely understood at this audience level; GPU is the higher-priority callout.

summary_soft_findings: §6 Implementation: no inline citation markers (figures derived from §5.4's cited content — not fabricated, but not traceable in §6 itself).
Sequence: 'model card' used without prior sequence introduction; explained inline.
Readability: 'GPU' not spelled out on first use.
